behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: catalog availability filter must override ranker output. Unit tests of filter or ranker alone can miss this interaction. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and no real AI/ML call is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real in-process modules (catalog source + filter + ranker). Seed products where an out-of-stock item has the highest relevance score. Assert final returned list excludes all out-of-stock products.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears when user-settings retrieval and category filtering are combined; single-function tests can miss mapping/serialization handoff errors. No real infra or AI-model dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process settings repository + recommender pipeline. Seed blocked categories in user settings and candidate products across categories. Assert blocked categories are absent from recommendations.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is algorithm scoring/ranking logic within one boundary; it does not require module interaction to fail. Rejected higher levels because behavior text focuses on ranking rule itself, not external infra or model-call quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking/scoring function with controlled feature vectors: products with strong recent-browse signal vs none. Assert score/order places browse-matching products above no-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Needs ranking + sort + pagination modules together; instability often appears at their boundary. Rejected System because text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess behavior; in-process wiring can expose ordering/pagination bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Build full in-process pipeline with deterministic fixture data. Request page 2 twice with unchanged state and assert identical item IDs/order and correct page slicing boundaries.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Serendipity is typically enforced after core ranking via diversification logic; failure appears when history analysis, candidate generation, and reranking interact. Rejected Agentic: behavior text describes deterministic recommendation logic, not a real AI/ML API call.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process pipeline with user history concentrated in one category and a mixed catalog. Assert top-N output includes at least one item from an unseen category while still respecting hard filters.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback depends on orchestration between profile-state detection and popularity-based candidate source. Unit tests can miss the handoff that incorrectly returns empty results.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process modules with a user having no history and a popularity dataset. Assert non-empty recommendations are returned and correspond to popularity ordering/fallback path.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at event-to-storage boundary: recommender response metadata must be correctly propagated into tracking records. Rejected System because this can be caught with in-process DB/repo and controllable clock without OS/network dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Serve recommendations, simulate a click on a known position, persist via tracking module to local test store, and assert stored product_id, rank_position, and timestamp fields are correct.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Latency SLA is a real runtime/infra property (CPU, memory, runtime behavior). Mocked or heavily isolated tests can pass while production-like execution fails. Behavior explicitly targets timing under scale.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end recommendation request in a production-like process with 100k-product dataset and representative user state; measure wall-clock latency (p95/p99 or strict bound per spec) and assert under 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Requires cross-module state handoff: purchase event ingestion/update plus subsequent ranking. Unit tests on ranker alone can miss stale-state propagation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, record purchase of a recommended product, then request recommendations again through in-process pipeline. Assert purchased product rank drops (or is removed) per business rule.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism risk usually emerges from interaction of candidate retrieval, sorting/tie-breakers, and pagination/state. Rejected Agentic because behavior text does not indicate non-deterministic LLM/AI model calls; rejected Workflow because no multi-agentic chain is described.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user state and fixed catalog snapshot, invoke full in-process recommendation pipeline twice and assert identical ordered product IDs across both runs.
